title: 10 best herbal teas for motion sickness

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava teas helps with motion sickness because its active compounds, kavalactones, have a natural calming effect on the body. These compounds interact with neurotransmitters in the brain to reduce anxiety and stress-related symptoms that often exacerbate motion sickness. By soothing the nervous system, kava tea can help alleviate queasiness, dizziness, and disorientation associated with motion sickness, allowing individuals to enjoy their travel or adventure without discomfort.
remedy_properties: Kavalactones: Kavalactones, the active compounds found in kava tea, have been shown to help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by binding to GABA receptors in the brain, reducing anxiety and calming the stomach.

Sedative properties: The sedative effects of kava tea can also help to reduce feelings of unease and discomfort caused by motion sickness, allowing the body to better adapt to changing surroundings and motions.

Antihistamine-like effects: Kava tea has been found to have antihistamine-like properties, which may help to block the release of histamine, a chemical that can exacerbate nausea and vomiting in response to motion stimuli.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in kavalactones, a group of compounds that have been shown to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Roots: Contain a variety of bioactive compounds that can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, such as anxiety and dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of piper methysticum in the morning for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade drying or air circulation methods within hours.
Combine one teaspoon of dried piper methysticum leaves with one cup of boiling water, steeping for five minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve and discard solids before adding honey to taste and serving warm.
Drink the prepared tea 30 minutes prior to traveling or as needed during motion sickness episodes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop teas helps with motion sickness because it contains compounds that have a natural affinity for calming the stomach and reducing nausea. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to alleviate the discomfort and bloating often associated with motion sickness. Additionally, maypop has been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce feelings of queasiness and dizziness, providing relief from symptoms that can make traveling or boating a miserable experience.
remedy_properties: Passionflower alkaloids: Help to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and nausea associated with motion sickness, by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain.
Apigenin: A flavonoid that acts as a natural antihistamine, helping to reduce the body's histamine response to motion sickness, which can alleviate symptoms such as dizziness and vomiting.
Saponins: Soothe the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms in the gut, which can contribute to nausea and stomach upset associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maypop plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with motion sickness.
Seeds: The seeds of the Maypop plant contain fatty acids and other nutrients that can help calm the stomach and reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried passionflower herb from a reputable source for this recipe.
Boil one liter of water in a large pot and let it simmer for five minutes.
Add the 20 grams of dried passionflower herb to the boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve, retaining the liquid and discarding solids.
Allow the tea to cool to room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container for refrigeration or freezing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with motion sickness because its unique combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils work together to calm the stomach and alleviate nausea. The tea's soothing properties help to reduce inflammation in the digestive system, which can contribute to symptoms of motion sickness. Additionally, the tea's ability to relax the body and mind can help to ease feelings of anxiety and unease that often accompany motion sickness, making it a natural and effective remedy for those who suffer from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Daucosterol: This compound has been shown to have anti-nausea properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by reducing vomiting and stomach upset.
Scopoletin: As a natural antispasmodic, scopoletin may help relax the stomach muscles and reduce the frequency and severity of nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Quercetin: This flavonoid has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms such as abdominal discomfort and bloating that often accompany motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have a calming effect on the stomach and can help soothe nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Barks: The barks contain compounds that can help reduce symptoms of motion sickness, such as dizziness and lightheadedness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ginkgo biloba leaves by purchasing them from a reputable supplier or growing your own plants.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves per cup of boiling water and place it in a teapot.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in the hot water for 5 to 7 minutes, allowing the flavorful compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool for 2 to 3 minutes before consuming, as recommended dosage is one to two cups three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with motion sickness because it contains compounds that have a natural calming effect on the body, particularly the digestive system. The soothing properties of valerian root help to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness, making it an effective remedy for alleviating symptoms. Additionally, valerian's relaxing properties can also help to ease anxiety and stress caused by motion sickness, promoting a sense of calmness and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine-like properties: Valerian tea helps to reduce histamine levels in the body, which can alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as nausea, dizziness, and vomiting.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects: The calming compounds in valerian tea, such as valepotriates, can help to reduce anxiety and stress related to travel or motion, making it easier to cope with motion sickness symptoms.

Gastroprotective properties: Valerian tea may also help to soothe and protect the stomach lining from irritation caused by motion sickness, reducing the risk of nausea, vomiting, and other gastrointestinal issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the valerian plant have been used for centuries to treat anxiety, restlessness, and insomnia, making them an effective part for alleviating symptoms of motion sickness.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes contain a high concentration of essential oils, which are responsible for their sedative properties, helping to calm nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried valeriana officinalis root and boiling water.
Measure one teaspoon of dried valerian root for every eight ounces of water.
Heat the water in a pot until it reaches a boil then reduce heat for five minutes.
Add the measured valerian root to the hot water allowing it to steep for ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with motion sickness because of its unique properties that target the digestive system. The menthol in peppermint tea calms the stomach muscles, reducing nausea and discomfort caused by motion. Additionally, peppermint's natural oils have a soothing effect on the gut, alleviating bloating and cramps that often accompany motion sickness. By promoting relaxation and easing digestive distress, herbal peppermint teas provide relief from the uncomfortable symptoms of motion sickness, making it an effective remedy for those prone to seasickness or car sickness.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's cooling effect helps to reduce nausea and vomiting by relaxing the digestive tract and calming the stomach, which can alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint tea, such as menthone and limonene, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe an upset stomach and relieve discomfort associated with motion sickness.

Gastrointestinal soothing compounds: Peppermint tea contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid that have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help to reduce symptoms of motion sickness such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint contain menthol, which helps to relax digestive tract muscles and reduce nausea.
Leaves: Peppermint leaves have a calming effect on the stomach and can help to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness and lightheadedness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves or purchase dried peppermint leaves from a reputable source.
Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves or two tablespoons of fresh peppermint leaves per eight ounce cup.
Heat filtered water in a tea kettle to a boil and then reduce heat to simmer for three to five minutes.
Steep the peppermint leaves in the hot water for seven to ten minutes allowing the flavors and oils to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with motion sickness because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. The active compound, gingerol, has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness. Additionally, ginger tea's warm and soothing qualities can help ease stomach discomfort and promote digestion, allowing travelers to feel more comfortable during turbulent rides or seasickness-inducing boat trips. By sipping on a cup of herbal ginger tea, individuals can alleviate symptoms and enjoy their journey with reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Digestive stimulants: Ginger's digestive stimulants, including gingerol and galanolactone, help to stimulate digestion, relieve stomach discomfort, and prevent nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness.

Vomiting inhibitors: The sesquiterpenes present in ginger tea, such as bisabolene and farnesene, have been shown to inhibit the vomiting center of the brain, reducing the severity and duration of motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome, also known as the underground stem, has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Roots: Ginger roots contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe digestive issues caused by motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-15 grams of dried zingiber officinale root and rinse it with filtered water.
Boil 500ml of fresh water in a large pot for 5-7 minutes and then reduce the heat.
Steep the rinsed ginger root in the boiling water for 5-10 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey to the tea to sweeten it and stir well before drinking.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with motion sickness because its calming properties work to soothe the digestive system and reduce feelings of nausea. The tea's natural antispasmodic effects also help to ease stomach cramps and discomfort, allowing the body to better absorb nutrients and fluids during travel. Additionally, the aroma of lavender has been shown to have a sedative effect on the brain, helping to calm anxiety and stress that can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_properties: Linalool: This terpene helps to calm the stomach and reduce nausea, making it easier for the body to digest food while on the move, thereby alleviating symptoms of motion sickness.

Linalyl acetate: This compound has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce feelings of anxiety and discomfort that often accompany motion sickness, allowing the body to relax and better adapt to changing environments.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as stomach cramps, bloating, and diarrhea, all of which are common complaints during motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant have a calming effect and can help to soothe stomach upset, making them a popular choice for treating motion sickness.
Flowers: The flowers of the English lavender plant contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in amounts of one teaspoon per cup.
Boil filtered water to a temperature of 200°f for 5-7 minutes before preparing tea.
Steep the dried lavender flowers in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove loose particles and solids.
Serve the tea warm or at room temperature, allowing it to steep for an additional 2-3 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi teas helps with motion sickness because of its natural ability to calm the nervous system. The tea's active compound, bacopa monnieri, has been shown to reduce anxiety and stress caused by motion-related stimuli. Additionally, brahmi's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate gastrointestinal discomfort often associated with motion sickness. Sedative and anti-anxiety effects: The herb Bacopa monnieri, commonly used in brahmi tea, has been shown to possess sedative and anxiolytic properties, which can help to calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of anxiety and unease that often accompany motion sickness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Brahmi tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to alleviate nausea and stomach discomfort by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and preventing further irritation.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried bacopa monnieri herb into a small bowl.
Boil fresh water in a tea kettle or pot and let it cool for three minutes.
Pour the cooled water over the dried herb in a teapot or cup.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes before straining it into a mug.
Add honey or lemon to taste and enjoy the herbal tea within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with motion sickness because it has a natural ability to calm the stomach and digestive system, reducing symptoms such as nausea and dizziness. The active compounds in fennel tea, including anethole and limonene, have been shown to stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, helping to alleviate feelings of queasiness and discomfort. Ferulic acid: A phytochemical present in fennel, ferulic acid has been found to have antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which may contribute to motion sickness.

Chlorogenic acid: Another phytochemical in fennel, chlorogenic acid has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects similar to anethole, which can help soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are commonly used due to their ability to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea associated with motion sickness.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have been traditionally used in herbal remedies for motion sickness because of their natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried fennel seeds into a clean glass infuser or teapot.
Bring two cups of filtered water to a boil in a large pot on the stovetop.
Add the fennel seeds to the boiling water and reduce heat to medium-low for five minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a large cup, discarding solids and let it cool slightly.
Drink the warm tea within 30 minutes of preparation or store it in the refrigerator for up to two days.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried chamomile flowers measure out one teaspoon per cup.

Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon teas helps with motion sickness because it contains a unique combination of active compounds that work together to soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea. The cinnamaldehyde present in Ceylon cinnamon has natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract caused by motion sickness. remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 grams of fresh cinnamomum verum bark and grind it into fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steep the ground cinnamon in 250 milliliters of boiling water for exactly 7 minutes and 30 seconds.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any remaining cinnamon solids.
Add honey to taste and stir well, dissolving the sweetness evenly throughout the tea.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature of around 80 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with motion sickness because of its active compound, curcumin, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. Antioxidants: Turmeric's high antioxidant content helps protect the body from free radical damage caused by inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms such as dizziness, lightheadedness, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcumin, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Roots: Contain volatile oils and other compounds that have been traditionally used to treat nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried turmeric root curcuma longa powder into a small saucepan.
Boil one cup of fresh filtered water in the saucepan and add the turmeric powder.
Reduce heat to a simmer for 5-7 minutes allowing the flavors to meld together.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup.
Steep the tea for an additional 2-3 minutes and serve warm or at room temperature.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat motion sickness with herbal teas is to brew a calming concoction of peppermint, ginger, and lemon balm. Peppermint's menthol helps relax the stomach, while ginger's active compound, gingerol, reduces nausea and vomiting. Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties soothe digestive issues. Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herbs in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink as needed. This tea combination provides natural relief from motion sickness symptoms without any harsh side effects.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to motion sickness that are treated with herbal teas are nausea, dizziness, and anxiety. Peppermint tea is a popular remedy for digestive issues and can help soothe an upset stomach. Ginger tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties and can ease nausea and vomiting. Lemon balm tea is calming and can help alleviate feelings of anxiety and restlessness, while chamomile tea promotes relaxation and reduces symptoms of insomnia. These herbal teas offer a natural and gentle approach to managing these common complaints.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for motion sickness are a natural and effective remedy to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness. These teas work by reducing nausea, dizziness, and vomiting associated with traveling or motion. Examples of herbal teas that help with motion sickness include ginger, peppermint, and chamomile, which have been shown to soothe the stomach and promote relaxation. By drinking these teas, individuals can enjoy travel and everyday activities without discomfort, improving their overall quality of life.
